Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>幫我檢視bridge的程式是否有問題</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-11</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
兩個 high severity findings 抽查屬實。以下是完整審查結果彙整。

## Bridge 程式碼審查結果

Workflow 三個視角（correctness / security / 質量+資源洩漏）審查完成，共 25 條 findings。⚠️ **對抗驗證階段撞到 session limit（2:30pm 重置）只完成 1/12**，其餘由我降級自審抽查——兩條 high 已親自用 grep/read 證實，其餘標記為「未經異源驗證」。

### 🔴 High（實測確認）

1. **取消 turn 的旗標是斷鏈死旗標** — `sessionManager.ts:277,850` + `run-prompt.ts`
   `_cancelledTurn` 全 repo 只有宣告和 `cancel()` 賦值兩處，**零讀取者**（我 grep 證實）。註解宣稱 runPrompt 的 finally 會據此跳過收尾，實際完全沒檢查。後果：`/cancel` 後使用者發新訊息，被取消的舊 turn 稍後返回時會用新 turn 的共享 buffer 做 final render、可能誤觸發 SCHEDULE/RELAY/SEND_FILE token、污染 history、`finally` 還會把新 turn 的 `onUpdate` 清掉殺死 streaming。

2. **並發 `get()` 會 spawn 重複 ACP 子進程且洩漏** — `sessionManager.ts:346-353`
   `get()` 沒有 in-progress 去重（我確認 create 路徑無任何 pending map），而 `create()` 內有多個秒級 await（loadFacts、LLM 壓縮、spawn 子進程）。message handler、ASK callback、specialist 背景注入等並發觸發時，兩個 create 各 spawn 一個子進程，後者覆蓋前者的 map entry，被覆蓋的子進程**永遠不會被 close**（exit handler 的身份比對恆 false）。

### 🟡 Medium（未經異源驗證，除 #5）

3. **Status server 全路由零認證** — `status-server.ts:88-315`（兩個視角獨立命中）：設計上要走 cloudflared/ngrok tunnel 給 Mini App，但 `/api/specialists/:name/facts`（私有記憶）、preamble、cancel mutation 全裸，也沒驗 Telegram initData HMAC。tunnel URL 外洩 = 私有 facts 可被整批匯出。
4. **Relay 入站無 peer 白名單** — `commands/relay.ts:736-833`：peer 身份完全信任訊息內文自稱的 `[RELAY:name]`，RELAY_DELEGATE 的 goal 直接餵進有完整 tool 權限的主 agent，構成跨信任邊界 prompt injection（實務受「bot 不能私訊 bot」緩解）。
5. ✅**（已異源驗證）parallel specialist 的 timeout 被誤分類為 failed** — `specialist.ts:570-574`，與 `runSpecialistTask` 的分類邏輯不一致。
6. **verifier retry 後 summary 沒重算** — `specialist.ts:538-565`：回傳的摘要是被 verifier 打槍的第一輪輸出，rawOutput 卻是 retry 後的。
7. **`switchProvider` 漏了 inflight 擋牆** — `sessionManager.ts:588-595`：switchAgent/switchSession/newSession 都有，唯獨 `/model` 切換沒有，turn 進行中會直接 close 舊 client 噴錯。
8. **`askRegistry` 無界成長** — `telegram-ui.ts:254`：唯一刪除點是按鈕被點擊，沒點的永久殘留，長駐數週穩定漏記憶體。
9. **telegram relay 忽略 targetBot 參數** — `relay.ts:150-266`：多 peer 時訊息靜默誤投給設定裡唯一的 target bot，group mirror 卻顯示錯誤的目的地。
10. **`acpClient.close()` 用 execSync taskkill 阻塞 event loop**，且不 reject pending → kill 失敗時 promise 永久懸掛、inflight 永不釋放。

### 🟢 Low（12 條，摘要）

- streaming token 防護漏洞：`SEND_FILE` 修復 regex 貪婪跨行誤吞文字；`hideTrailingUnterminatedToken` 白名單漏 `MOA_PLAN`/`SPECIALIST_PROXY`（新 token 沒同步更新的同類掃描漏接）
- 資源洩漏模式：`Promise.race` timeout timer 四處從不 clearTimeout；`atomicJson` 的 writePending map 只增不減；`testRedisConnection` 會永久覆寫運行中 relay cfg
- relay 去重 key 只取前 100 字，連續 RELAY_RESULT JSON 可能被誤丟
- sticker emotionVectors 暖機失敗會永久快取空結果
- pinned-agent.json fire-and-forget 載入，啟動初期首訊息可能用錯 backend
- 死碼：`run-prompt.ts:1378` 不可達分支、tool-hooks 整套無呼叫者、ts-prune 一批 dead exports（`shutdown()` 從未接上三個 stop* 函式）
- BC-x 測試對映缺口：60+ smoke script 只有 2 個標了 BC-x

### 建議優先序

#1、#2 是實際會咬人的併發 bug（/cancel + 快速發訊、並發觸發點都真實存在），建議優先修。#3 在你開 tunnel 給 Mini App 之前必須補認證。其餘 medium 可批次處理，low 多數是「提及不動」的既有債。

要我修的話說一聲修哪幾條；若想先把剩下 11 條 findings 補完異源驗證，2:30pm limit 重置後可以 resume workflow（`resumeFromRunId: wf_442ed739-7ce`，已完成的 review 會走 cache 不重跑）。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

   `get()` 沒有 in-progress 去重（我確認 create 路徑無任何 pending map），而 `create()` 內有多個秒級 await（loadFacts、LLM 壓縮、spawn 子進程）。message handler、ASK callback、specialist 背景注入等並發觸發時，兩個 create 各 spawn 一個子進程，後者覆蓋前者的 map entry，被覆蓋的子進程**永遠不會被 close**（exit handler 的身份比對恆 false）。

### 🟡 Medium（未經異源驗證，除 #5）

3. **Status server 全路由零認證** — `status-server.ts:88-315`（兩個視角獨立命中）：設計上要走 cloudflared/ngrok tunnel 給 Mini App，但 `/api/specialists/:name/facts`（私有記憶）、preamble、cancel mutation 全裸，也沒驗 Telegram initData HMAC。tunnel URL 外洩 = 私有 facts 可被整批匯出。
4. **Relay 入站無 peer 白名單** — `commands/relay.ts:736-833`：peer 身份完全信任訊息內文自稱的 `[RELAY:name]`，RELAY_DELEGATE 的 goal 直接餵進有完整 tool 權限的主 agent，構成跨信任邊界 prompt injection（實務受「bot 不能私訊 bot」緩解）。
5. ✅**（已異源驗證）parallel specialist 的 timeout 被誤分類為 failed** — `specialist.ts:570-574`，與 `runSpecialistTask` 的分類邏輯不一致。
6. **verifier retry 後 summary 沒重算** — `specialist.ts:538-565`：回傳的摘要是被 verifier 打槍的第一輪輸出，rawOutput 卻是 retry 後的。
7. **`switchProvider` 漏了 inflight 擋牆** — `sessionManager.ts:588-595`：switchAgent/switchSession/newSession 都有，唯獨 `/model` 切換沒有，turn 進行中會直接 close 舊 client 噴錯。
8. **`askRegistry` 無界成長** — `telegram-ui.ts:254`：唯一刪除點是按鈕被點擊，沒點的永久殘留，長駐數週穩定漏記憶體。
9. **telegram relay 忽略 targetBot 參數** — `relay.ts:150-266`：多 peer 時訊息靜默誤投給設定裡唯一的 target bot，group mirror 卻顯示錯誤的目的地。
10. **`acpClient.close()` 用 execSync taskkill 阻塞 event loop**，且不 reject pending → kill 失敗時 promise 永久懸掛、inflight 永不釋放。

### 🟢 Low（12 條，摘要）

- streaming token 防護漏洞：`SEND_FILE` 修復 regex 貪婪跨行誤吞文字；`hideTrailingUnterminatedToken` 白名單漏 `MOA_PLAN`/`SPECIALIST_PROXY`（新 token 沒同步更新的同類掃描漏接）
- 資源洩漏模式：`Promise.race` timeout timer 四處從不 clearTimeout；`atomicJson` 的 writePending map 只增不減；`testRedisConnection` 會永久覆寫運行中 relay cfg
- relay 去重 key 只取前 100 字，連續 RELAY_RESULT JSON 可能被誤丟
- sticker emotionVectors 暖機失敗會永久快取空結果
- pinned-agent.json fire-and-forget 載入，啟動初期首訊息可能用錯 backend
- 死碼：`run-prompt.ts:1378` 不可達分支、tool-hooks 整套無呼叫者、ts-prune 一批 dead exports（`shutdown()` 從未接上三個 stop* 函式）
- BC-x 測試對映缺口：60+ smoke script 只有 2 個標了 BC-x

### 建議優先序

#1、#2 是實際會咬人的併發 bug（/cancel + 快速發訊、並發觸發點都真實存在），建議優先修。#3 在你開 tunnel 給 Mini App 之前必須補認證。其餘 medium 可批次處理，low 多數是「提及不動」的既有債。

要我修的話說一聲修哪幾條；若想先把剩下 11 條 findings 補完異源驗證，2:30pm limit 重置後可以 resume workflow（`resumeFromRunId: wf_442ed739-7ce`，已完成的 review 會走 cache 不重跑）。

<summary>
  <request>Code review of bridge program for issues and bugs</request>
  
  <investigated>Multi-perspective code review of bridge codebase completed using workflow with three dimensions: correctness, security, and quality/resource-leak analysis. Review examined sessionManager.ts, run-prompt.ts, status-server.ts, relay commands, specialist management, and Telegram UI components. 25 findings identified across high/medium/low severity levels. Adversarial verification phase hit session limits (only 1/12 completed before 2:30pm reset), with two high-severity findings manually verified via grep/read.</investigated>
  
  <learned>Two critical concurrency bugs confirmed in bridge: (1) _cancelledTurn flag in sessionManager.ts is a dead flag - declared and set but never read, causing cancelled turns to pollute shared buffers and kill new turn streaming when they return late; (2) sessionManager.get() lacks in-progress deduplication, allowing concurrent calls to spawn duplicate ACP subprocesses that leak when map entries get overwritten. Status server routes (/api/specialists/:name/facts, preamble, cancel mutations) completely lack authentication despite handling private memory data. Relay inbound trusts peer identity from message content without whitelist validation. Multiple resource leak patterns found: Promise.race timeouts never cleared, atomicJson writePending map grows unbounded, askRegistry never cleaned up for unclicked buttons.</learned>
  
  <completed>Code review completed with 25 findings catalogued: 2 high-severity (both verified), 8 medium-severity (1 verified, 7 pending adversarial verification), 12 low-severity issues covering streaming token vulnerabilities, resource leak patterns, relay deduplication weaknesses, dead code, and test coverage gaps. Findings documented with file locations, failure scenarios, and priority recommendations.</completed>
  
  <next_steps>Awaiting user decision on which findings to fix first (recommendation: prioritize high-severity #1 cancelled turn flag and #2 concurrent get() subprocess leak). Option to resume workflow verification after 2:30pm session limit reset using resumeFromRunId to complete adversarial verification of remaining 11 findings.</next_steps>
  
  <notes>Session limit interrupted full adversarial verification process, leaving majority of findings in "self-reviewed but not independently verified" state. The two high-severity concurrency bugs have real triggering scenarios (cancel + rapid new message, concurrent session access points) and should be addressed before tunnel exposure for Mini App, which would make the authentication-less status server routes exploitable.</notes>
</summary>
```

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\sessionManager.ts\",\"content\":\"\\n  resetTurn(session: ChatSession) {\\n    // Flush any pending orphan output before a new live turn clears the\\n    // buffers, so background continuation isn't lost or interleaved with the\\n    // upcoming turn's streaming placeholder.\\n    if (session._orphanBuffer || session._orphanFlushTimer) this.flushOrphan(session);\\n    session._orphanSuppressUntil = undefined;\\n    session.buffer = \\\"\\\";\\n    session.thinkingBuffer = undefined;\\n    session.toolLog = [];\\n    session.toolTimeline = [];\\n    session.stderrTail = \\\"\\\";\\n    session._toolInFlight = false;\\n    session._streamingDelegates = undefined;\\n    session._toolTitles?.clear();\\n    // push turn 邊界 marker 但「不清空」_recentToolNames——硬清空會讓每 turn 第一個\\n    // tool 的 precedingTools 永遠空；marker 讓下游 analyzer 自己切斷跨 turn combo。\\n    if (session._recentToolNames?.length) {\\n      session._recentToolNames = appendToolName(session._recentToolNames, TURN_MARKER);\\n    }\\n    this.startInactivityTimer(session);\\n  }\\n\\n  cancel(chatId: number) {\\n    const s = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (!s) return;\\n    s.client.cancel();\\n\\n    // Optimistic cancel: immediately free the session so the user can send\\n    // new messages without waiting for the CLI to acknowledge cancellation.\\n    s._cancelledTurn = true;\\n    s.inflight = false;\\n    s._toolInFlight = false;\\n    s.onUpdate = undefined;\\n    this.clearInactivityTimer(s);\\n    // Drop any orphan capture in progress: after cancel, inflight=false means\\n    // trailing chunks from the cancelled turn would flip to the orphan path.\\n    // A cancel means \\\"stop, I don't want this\\\" — discard rather than flush\\n    // abandoned partial output. Genuine later background output re-arms fresh.\\n    if (s._orphanFlushTimer) { clearTimeout(s._orphanFlushTimer); s._orphanFlushTimer = undefined; }\\n    s._orphanBuffer = undefined;\\n    // Suppress capture across the force-kill window so trailing chunks don't leak.\\n    s._orphanSuppressUntil = Date.now() + 20_000;\\n\\n    // Force-kill escalation: if CLI doesn't exit the turn within 15s,\\n    // kill the process tree and re-create on next message.\\n    if (!s.client.isClosed) {\\n      const timer = setTimeout(async () => {\\n        if (s.client.isClosed) return;\\n        console.warn(`[cancel] chat ${chatId} CLI still alive after 15s — force-killing`);\\n        await s.client.close();\\n      }, 15_000);\\n      timer.unref?.();\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Enable drop-all mode: silently discard messages sent before now. */\\n  enableDropAll(chatId: number) { this._dropBefore.set(chatId, Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)); }\\n  /** Disable drop-all mode. */\\n  disableDropAll(chatId: number) { this._dropBefore.delete(chatId); }\",\"numLines\":60,\"startLine\":820,\"totalLines\":1128}}"</outcome>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Cancellation flag _cancelledTurn declared but potentially unused</title>
  <subtitle>SessionManager defines _cancelledTurn flag with documentation claiming run-prompt finally block checks it, verification in progress</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>sessionManager.ts line 277-278 declares _cancelledTurn boolean flag with comment "set true when cancel() fires; run-prompt finally skips post-processing"</fact>
    <fact>ChatSession interface contains 20+ internal flags for turn state management including inflight, _toolInFlight, _orphanBuffer, _orphanSuppressUntil</fact>
    <fact>_orphanSuppressUntil uses epoch-ms suppression window to drop trailing chunks from cancelled turns after inflight flips false</fact>
    <fact>SessionManager.get() method returns existing session if client not closed and updates lastUsed timestamp, otherwise calls create()</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Investigation of bridge cancellation mechanism examining sessionManager.ts interface definition. The _cancelledTurn flag is documented as being set when cancel() fires, with the expectation that run-prompt's finally block will check it to skip post-processing. The session manager maintains extensive internal state for turn lifecycle including inflight tracking, tool call status, orphan turn buffering, and cancellation suppression windows. The _orphanSuppressUntil mechanism uses epoch timestamps to drop chunks arriving after cancellation, suggesting there is some cancellation handling infrastructure in place. This reading is part of verifying a reported finding that _cancelledTurn may be a "dead flag" - declared and set but never actually read by the code that should be checking it.</narrative>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\sessionManager.ts\",\"content\":\"\\n  resetTurn(session: ChatSession) {\\n    // Flush any pending orphan output before a new live turn clears the\\n    // buffers, so background continuation isn't lost or interleaved with the\\n    // upcoming turn's streaming placeholder.\\n    if (session._orphanBuffer || session._orphanFlushTimer) this.flushOrphan(session);\\n    session._orphanSuppressUntil = undefined;\\n    session.buffer = \\\"\\\";\\n    session.thinkingBuffer = undefined;\\n    session.toolLog = [];\\n    session.toolTimeline = [];\\n    session.stderrTail = \\\"\\\";\\n    session._toolInFlight = false;\\n    session._streamingDelegates = undefined;\\n    session._toolTitles?.clear();\\n    // push turn 邊界 marker 但「不清空」_recentToolNames——硬清空會讓每 turn 第一個\\n    // tool 的 precedingTools 永遠空；marker 讓下游 analyzer 自己切斷跨 turn combo。\\n    if (session._recentToolNames?.length) {\\n      session._recentToolNames = appendToolName(session._recentToolNames, TURN_MARKER);\\n    }\\n    this.startInactivityTimer(session);\\n  }\\n\\n  cancel(chatId: number) {\\n    const s = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (!s) return;\\n    s.client.cancel();\\n\\n    // Optimistic cancel: immediately free the session so the user can send\\n    // new messages without waiting for the CLI to acknowledge cancellation.\\n    s._cancelledTurn = true;\\n    s.inflight = false;\\n    s._toolInFlight = false;\\n    s.onUpdate = undefined;\\n    this.clearInactivityTimer(s);\\n    // Drop any orphan capture in progress: after cancel, inflight=false means\\n    // trailing chunks from the cancelled turn would flip to the orphan path.\\n    // A cancel means \\\"stop, I don't want this\\\" — discard rather than flush\\n    // abandoned partial output. Genuine later background output re-arms fresh.\\n    if (s._orphanFlushTimer) { clearTimeout(s._orphanFlushTimer); s._orphanFlushTimer = undefined; }\\n    s._orphanBuffer = undefined;\\n    // Suppress capture across the force-kill window so trailing chunks don't leak.\\n    s._orphanSuppressUntil = Date.now() + 20_000;\\n\\n    // Force-kill escalation: if CLI doesn't exit the turn within 15s,\\n    // kill the process tree and re-create on next message.\\n    if (!s.client.isClosed) {\\n      const timer = setTimeout(async () => {\\n        if (s.client.isClosed) return;\\n        console.warn(`[cancel] chat ${chatId} CLI still alive after 15s — force-killing`);\\n        await s.client.close();\\n      }, 15_000);\\n      timer.unref?.();\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Enable drop-all mode: silently discard messages sent before now. */\\n  enableDropAll(chatId: number) { this._dropBefore.set(chatId, Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)); }\\n  /** Disable drop-all mode. */\\n  disableDropAll(chatId: number) { this._dropBefore.delete(chatId); }\",\"numLines\":60,\"startLine\":820,\"totalLines\":1128}}"</outcome>

content.thinking:
The user is observing the primary session where they're investigating the _cancelledTurn flag issue. Let me track what's being discovered:

1. First read (lines 250-360 of sessionManager.ts): Shows the _cancelledTurn flag declaration with the comment "set true when cancel() fires; run-prompt finally skips post-processing"

2. Second read (lines 820-880 of sessionManager.ts): Shows the cancel() method which SETS _cancelledTurn = true (line 850), along with other cleanup

3. Third read (lines 960-1090 of run-prompt.ts): Shows the relay reply path and Telegram message delivery - no check for _cancelledTurn visible

4. Fourth read (lines 1620-1710 of run-prompt.ts): Shows the finally block that clears session.onUpdate, sets inflight=false, drains pending meta queue, and handles continuation plans - but NO CHECK for _cancelledTurn

This is confirming the bug: the flag is SET in cancel() but never READ in the finally block that should be checking it. The finally block does all the post-processing (drain meta queue, schedule continuations) without checking if the turn was cancelled.

This is a discovery/confirmation of a bug.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Cancelled turn flag never checked in run-prompt finally block</title>
  <subtitle>cancel() sets _cancelledTurn=true but run-prompt.ts finally block never reads it, allowing post-processing to proceed</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>sessionManager.ts line 850 sets _cancelledTurn=true in cancel() method with comment claiming run-prompt finally will skip post-processing</fact>
    <fact>run-prompt.ts lines 1660-1710 finally block clears onUpdate, sets inflight=false, drains _pendingMetaQueue and schedules continuation turns without checking _cancelledTurn</fact>
    <fact>cancel() method immediately sets inflight=false and clears onUpdate to free session for new messages without waiting for CLI acknowledgment</fact>
    <fact>Post-processing in finally block includes draining queued meta prompts with 500ms delay and scheduling multi-turn continuation plans</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Bug confirmed in bridge cancellation flow: the _cancelledTurn flag exists as documented dead code. When user invokes /cancel, sessionManager.cancel() sets session._cancelledTurn=true, flips inflight to false, and clears onUpdate to immediately free the session for new user messages. However, the run-prompt.ts finally block (lines 1660-1710) performs complete post-processing - draining pending meta prompt queue, scheduling continuation turns - without ever checking whether _cancelledTurn is true. The flag is set but has zero readers. This creates a race condition: if user sends new message after cancel, the cancelled turn's late completion will execute its finally block using the new turn's shared session state (buffer, onUpdate callback), potentially flushing wrong content, triggering relay/file tokens meant for cancelled work, and clearing the new turn's onUpdate to kill its streaming.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
    <file>src/run-prompt.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

content.thinking:
The user is examining run-prompt.ts to understand the flow around session.inflight = true, resetTurn, onUpdate, and the prompt execution. They're verifying the bug that _cancelledTurn is never checked.

From the observations:
1. Line 352: session.inflight = true
2. Line 353: sessions.resetTurn(session)
3. Line 562: session.onUpdate = ... (setting the update callback)
4. Line 601: await session.client.prompt(promptText) - this is the main execution
5. Line 1670: session.onUpdate = undefined (in the finally block)

The flow is:
- Set inflight=true
- Reset turn buffers
- Set onUpdate callback
- Call client.prompt() 
- In finally block: clear onUpdate, set inflight=false

The key finding is that there's NO check of _cancelledTurn before the finally block does post-processing (draining pending meta queue, scheduling continuations).

This confirms the bug: cancel() sets _cancelledTurn=true and inflight=false immediately to free the session, but when the cancelled turn eventually completes, its finally block runs without checking _cancelledTurn, so it can interfere with a new turn that has started.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Run-prompt lifecycle shows inflight flag shared between overlapping turns</title>
  <subtitle>Cancelled turn sets inflight=false immediately to free session, but new turn reuses same session object and shared buffers</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>run-prompt.ts line 352 sets session.inflight=true at turn start, line 353 calls sessions.resetTurn() to clear buffer/toolLog/toolTimeline</fact>
    <fact>Line 562 assigns session.onUpdate callback for streaming updates, line 601 executes await session.client.prompt(promptText)</fact>
    <fact>Finally block at line 1670 sets session.onUpdate=undefined and line 1671 sets session.inflight=false after draining _pendingMetaQueue</fact>
    <fact>sessionManager.cancel() sets inflight=false and clears onUpdate immediately without waiting for prompt() to complete or finally block to run</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Discovery of the race condition mechanism in run-prompt lifecycle. When cancel() fires, it immediately sets inflight=false and clears onUpdate to let user send new messages without waiting. The new message enters runPrompt(), sees inflight=false, proceeds to set inflight=true again, calls resetTurn() to clear buffers, assigns a new onUpdate callback, and starts its own prompt(). Meanwhile, the cancelled turn's await client.prompt() eventually completes (or errors out) and reaches its finally block. That finally block has no awareness that the session has been reused - it accesses session.buffer (now containing the new turn's streaming content), clears session.onUpdate (killing the new turn's streaming), and drains _pendingMetaQueue using the current session state. The session object is shared, not cloned per turn, so both turns mutate the same buffer/onUpdate/toolLog fields.</narrative>

content.thinking:
The user is showing me grep results for `turnFinalized|unregisterRecovery|registerRecovery` in run-prompt.ts. This is continuing the investigation into the cancellation bug.

Key findings from this grep:
1. Line 444: `let turnFinalized = false;` - this is a local variable, not a session property
2. Lines 480, 504, 528, 537, 548: Multiple checks of `turnFinalized` to prevent operations after turn is done
3. Line 606: `turnFinalized = true;` set after the prompt() completes
4. Line 547: `unregisterRecovery` is defined as a function
5. Line 1669: `unregisterRecovery()` is called in the finally block

The important insight here is that `turnFinalized` is a LOCAL variable in the runPrompt function scope, not a property on the session object. This means:
- Each runPrompt() call has its own `turnFinalized` flag
- When a turn is cancelled and a new turn starts, the NEW turn has its own fresh `turnFinalized = false`
- But the OLD cancelled turn ALSO has its own `turnFinalized` in its function scope
- The cancelled turn's `turnFinalized` will become `true` when its prompt() completes (line 606)
- Then its finally block runs with its own local flags

So the problem is:
1. Old turn is running with `turnFinalized = false`
2. Cancel fires, sets session.inflight=false, clears session.onUpdate
3. User sends new message, new runPrompt() starts with its own fresh `turnFinalized = false`
4. New turn sets session.inflight=true, session.onUpdate = newCallback, resets session.buffer
5. Old turn's prompt() eventually returns, sets its local `turnFinalized = true`
6. Old turn's finally block runs, sees its own `turnFinalized = true`, proceeds with cleanup
7. Old turn's finally clears session.onUpdate (which is now the NEW turn's callback!)

The local `turnFinalized` flag doesn't protect against the session-level race condition because each turn has its own copy of this variable. This is the core issue—the flag is scoped to individual turns but the session state is shared across all turns.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>turnFinalized is local variable not session property, doesn't prevent cross-turn interference</title>
  <subtitle>Each runPrompt call has own turnFinalized flag, cancelled turn's finally block uses its own flag while accessing shared session state</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>run-prompt.ts line 444 declares turnFinalized as local variable in runPrompt function scope, not as session property</fact>
    <fact>Line 606 sets turnFinalized=true after prompt() completes, lines 480/504/528/537/548 check it to suppress operations after completion</fact>
    <fact>Line 547 defines unregisterRecovery callback with turnFinalized check, line 1669 calls unregisterRecovery() in finally block</fact>
    <fact>Finally block at lines 1669-1672 calls unregisterRecovery(), clears session.onUpdate, sets session.inflight=false after turnFinalized guards pass</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Critical discovery about the scope of turn completion tracking. The turnFinalized variable is declared as a local variable within each runPrompt() function invocation, not as a property on the shared session object. This means when a turn is cancelled and a new turn starts, there are now TWO separate runPrompt() executions each with their own independent turnFinalized flag. The cancelled turn's local turnFinalized=false will eventually become true when its prompt() returns (line 606), at which point its finally block executes cleanup logic using its own local flag checks. However, the finally block's cleanup operations (unregisterRecovery, session.onUpdate=undefined, session.inflight=false) mutate the SHARED session object. So the cancelled turn's finally block sees turnFinalized=true (its own local copy), passes all the guards that check turnFinalized, and proceeds to clear session.onUpdate - which by this time has been reassigned to the new turn's streaming callback. The local scope of turnFinalized provides no protection against this cross-turn session state interference.</narrative>

content.thinking:
The user is editing sessionManager.ts to add a line that resets `_cancelledTurn` to false in the `resetTurn` method. This is fixing the bug that was discovered earlier where the _cancelledTurn flag is set but never checked, and now they're making sure it gets reset at the start of each new turn.

This is a bugfix being applied to address the high-severity finding about the cancelled turn flag being a dead flag. By adding `session._cancelledTurn = false;` in resetTurn(), they're ensuring that when a new turn starts, the cancellation flag from any previous turn is cleared.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Reset _cancelledTurn flag at start of each new turn</title>
  <subtitle>Added session._cancelledTurn=false in resetTurn() to clear cancellation state when new turn begins</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>sessionManager.ts line 827 now sets session._cancelledTurn=false in resetTurn() method</fact>
    <fact>resetTurn() is called at start of each turn from run-prompt.ts line 353 to clear transient turn state</fact>
    <fact>This ensures cancelled turn flag from previous turn does not persist into new turn execution</fact>
    <fact>Flag is reset alongside other turn-scoped state like buffer, toolLog, toolTimeline, _orphanSuppressUntil</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>First step of fixing the cancelled turn race condition bug. The _cancelledTurn flag is now explicitly reset to false in resetTurn(), which is called at the start of each new turn. This ensures that when a user sends a new message after cancelling a previous one, the new turn starts with _cancelledTurn=false rather than inheriting the true value set by cancel(). This prevents the new turn from being incorrectly treated as cancelled. The flag is reset in the same block that clears other per-turn state like buffer, toolLog, and _orphanSuppressUntil, maintaining consistency with the turn lifecycle. However, this is only a partial fix - the finally block in run-prompt.ts still needs to check this flag before performing cleanup operations on shared session state.</narrative>

  </files_modified>
</observation>

If the file doesn't\\n    // exist or is corrupt we just start fresh (same as before).\\n    loadPinnedAgents().then((map) => {\\n      for (const [k, v] of map) {\\n        if (!this.acpBackendByChat.has(k)) this.acpBackendByChat.set(k, v);\\n      }\\n    }).catch(() => {});\\n  }\\n\\n  async get(chatId: number, userId?: number): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    const existing = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (existing && !existing.client.isClosed) {\\n      existing.lastUsed = Date.now();\\n      return existing;\\n    }\\n    return this.create(chatId, userId);\\n  }\\n\\n  private async create(chatId: number, userId?: number, providerType?: ProviderType): Promise<ChatSession> {\\n    // Inject TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID so MCP children (mcp-memory) know whose\\n    // facts file to write. Each chat owns its own Kiro ACP subprocess,\\n * so conversations stay isolated.\\n */\\nexport interface ChatSession {\\n  chatId: number;\\n  userId?: number;\\n  client: Provider;\\n  /** Which provider type is active for this session. */\\n  activeProvider: ProviderType;\\n  /**\\n   * Which ACP backend (acp-providers.json key) this session's subprocess was\\n   * spawned from. undefined = the .env agent (no /agent switch yet) or a\\n   * non-ACP provider.\\n   */\\n  acpBackend?: string;\\n  sessionId: string;\\n  lastUsed: number;\\n  /** Accumulated assistant text for the in-flight turn. */\\n  buffer: string;\\n  /** Accumulated thinking text for the in-flight turn (local model only). */\\n  thinkingBuffer?: string;\\n  /**\\n   * Non-tool status lines for the in-flight turn (e.g. reflexion hints).\\n   * Per-tool progress now lives in {@link toolTimeline}; this only carries\\n   * the extra advisory lines that render after the tool list.\\n   */\\n  toolLog: string[];\\n  /**\\n   * Per-tool progress for the in-flight turn. One entry per tool call,\\n   * updated in place (running → completed/failed) so the streamed message\\n   * shows one line per tool instead of a start+finish pair.\\n   */\\n  toolTimeline: ToolEvent[];\\n  /** Rolling buffer of the agent's latest stderr output. */\\n  stderrTail: string;\\n  /** Called whenever new streamed text is appended. */\\n  onUpdate?: (session: ChatSession) => void;\\n  /** Conversation history for /save export. */\\n  history: HistoryEntry[];\\n  /** Cumulative characters sent by user (approximate context-in). */\\n  charsIn: number;\\n  /** Cumulative characters emitted by agent (approximate context-out). */\\n  charsOut: number;\\n  /** Latest usage meta reported by agent (tokens, etc.), if any. */\\n  usage?: Record<string, unknown>;\\n  /** Long-term memory preamble to inject on first prompt. */\\n  memoryPreamble: string;\\n  /**\\n   * Char-length breakdown of `memoryPreamble`. Captured once at session\\n   * create so the /context and streaming-footer UI can show where the\\n   * preamble tokens are going (tool notes / env block / memory block)\\n   * without having to re-measure the string.\\n   */\\n  preambleBreakdown: {\\n    total: number;\\n    toolNotes: number;\\n    envBlock: number;\\n    memoryBlock: number;\\n    dailylogBlock: number;\\n  };\\n  /** Set true after first prompt so we inject preamble only once. */\\n  preambleInjected: boolean;\\n  /**\\n   * True when this session was restored via ACP session/load（方案 A resume）。\\n   * Preamble 凍在原 agent session 內、bridge 本 process 未重注，\\n   * breakdown 歸零是刻意的——/context、/usage 用此 flag 加註說明。\\n   */\\n  resumed?: boolean;\\n  /** Stripped tail facts from preamble — used by memory-recall dedup. */\\n  tailFacts: Set<string>;\\n  /** Wiki slugs already injected this session — skip on subsequent turns. */\\n  injectedWikiSlugs: Set<string>;\\n  /** Skill names already injected this session — skip on subsequent turns. */\\n  injectedSkills: Set<string>;\\n  /**\\n   * True while a session/prompt call is in-flight against this session's\\n   * ACP client. kiro-cli rejects concurrent prompts with\\n   * `-32603 Prompt already in progress`; callers must check this flag\\n   * before calling `client.prompt()` and bail out with a friendly message\\n   * instead of racing.\\n   */\\n  inflight: boolean;\\n  /** Internal: inactivity timer handle for the current turn. */\\n  _inactivityTimer?: NodeJS.Timeout;\\n  /** Internal: true while a tool_call has started but not yet completed/failed. */\\n  _toolInFlight?: boolean;\\n  /** Internal: RELAY_DELEGATE tokens captured during streaming (workaround for CLI stripping tokens from final text). */\\n  _streamingDelegates?: string[];\\n  /** Path of the most recent user upload in this turn (consumed by RELAY_DELEGATE auto-attach). */\\n  _lastUploadPath?: string;\\n  /** Internal: meta prompts queued while session was inflight (e.g. parallel delegate results). Drained after inflight=false. */\\n  _pendingMetaQueue: Array<{ ctx: unknown; prompt: string; opts: Record<string, unknown> }>;\\n  /** Internal: consecutive tool call failures for reflexion hint injection. */\\n  _consecutiveToolFails: number;\\n  /**\\n   * Internal: 把 tool_call 起始時的 human-readable title 綁到 opaque toolCallId，\\n   * 供 tool_call_update（只帶 id 不帶 name）解析工具名。用完即刪。\\n   */\\n  _toolTitles?: Map<string, string>;\\n  /**\\n   * Internal: rolling 純工具名序列（含 turn 邊界 marker），供 Tier A seq observation 的\\n   * precedingTools。僅在 tool_call case 維護一處 push；resetTurn 推入 TURN_MARKER 不清空。\\n   */\\n  _recentToolNames?: string[];\\n  /** Internal: set true when cancel() fires; run-prompt finally skips post-processing. */\\n  _cancelledTurn?: boolean;\\n  /** Internal: message_id of the current turn's placeholder (for cancel UI feedback). */\\n  _placeholderMsgId?: number;\\n  /** Internal: true once the 70% context budget warning has been injected this session. */\\n  _budgetWarned?: boolean;\\n  /**\\n   * Internal: accumulates agent_message_chunk text that arrives while NO\\n   * prompt turn is in-flight (an \\\"orphan turn\\\" — typically a Claude Code\\n   * harness background sub-agent completing and re-invoking the agent via\\n   * task-notification). Flushed by the orphan-turn-flusher.\\n   */\\n  _orphanBuffer?: string;\\n  /** Internal: debounce timer for the orphan-turn flush (armed per chunk). */\\n  _orphanFlushTimer?: NodeJS.Timeout;\\n  /**\\n   * Internal: epoch-ms until which orphan capture is suppressed.
